title: T-006 — Migration: routings and routing_operations tables
prompt:
# T-006 — Migration: routings and routing_operations tables

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.6, §12.1, §12.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Routings + routing_operations with cascading delete and resource FKs.

FK to production_lines and machines comes from 02-SETTINGS — if those tables exist, declare hard FK. If not present in test fixture, declare soft (UUID) and document in ADR-update note. manufacturing_operation_name pairs with bom_lines field of same name for cross-link queries.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_routing.sql [create]
- db/schema/routing.ts [create]
- tests/db/routing.migration.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Routing API (T-022)
- Routing UI (T-051..054)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration applied, when describing routing_operations, then UNIQUE(routing_id,op_no) is enforced.
2. Given a routings row deleted, when querying routing_operations, then dependent rows are removed via CASCADE.
3. Given two versions for same item exist, when UNIQUE(org_id,item_id,version) constraint is hit, then duplicate fails.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for unique constraints + cascade.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/routing.migration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not pre-seed any routings or operations.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
